soilPreparation.title: Essential Tips for Soil Preparation
soilPreparation.excerpt: Learn the professional secrets to preparing your soil for the upcoming farming season to maximize yield...
soilPreparation.author: Admin
points: Choose the right tiller for your soil type.
Remove weeds and leftover crop residue before planting.
Maintain proper soil moisture before tilling.
Use cultivators for better aeration and root growth.
Break large soil clods before seed placement.
Level the field to avoid waterlogging in low spots.
Add compost or organic manure before final cultivation.
Avoid working soil when it is too wet or sticky.
Check soil pH and correct nutrient imbalance early.
Prepare raised beds for vegetables and delicate crops.
Keep tiller blades clean and sharp for uniform soil mixing.
Use shallow cultivation around existing roots.
Plan drainage channels before the rainy season.
Rotate crops to maintain soil health.
Do a final light pass before sowing for smooth seed beds.

powerTools.title: Maintaining Your Power Tools
powerTools.excerpt: A comprehensive guide on how to clean and maintain your industrial power tools for longevity and safety...
powerTools.author: Agri Expert
points: Clean tools after every use.
Remove soil, dust, and crop residue from blades.
Check engine oil before starting.
Clean or replace air filters regularly.
Tighten loose nuts, bolts, and screws.
Sharpen blades when cutting performance decreases.
Check fuel lines for leakage.
Store tools in a dry and shaded place.
Lubricate moving parts regularly.
Inspect power cables and switches.
Replace damaged blades immediately.
Use only recommended fuel or power source.
Avoid overloading the machine.
Follow manufacturer safety instructions.
Service the tool at regular intervals.

irrigation.title: Modern Irrigation Techniques
irrigation.excerpt: Exploring the latest water-saving irrigation systems that are revolutionizing modern agriculture in India...
irrigation.author: Editor
points: Use drip irrigation to save water in row crops.
Maintain pumps and filters regularly.
Choose quality hoses, pipes, and connectors.
Monitor water pressure during irrigation.
Install filters before drip lines to reduce clogging.
Flush drip pipes at regular intervals.
Use sprinklers for even coverage in open fields.
Select pump capacity based on field size and head height.
Repair leaks quickly to prevent water loss.
Schedule irrigation early morning or evening.
Group crops with similar water needs together.
Use rain guns for larger field coverage.
Check nozzles for blockage before every cycle.
Protect pipes from direct damage during field operations.
Track soil moisture before increasing watering time.
